tables.description: Tracks medical certificates held by crew members, including issuance and expiry dates and current validity status for fitness-to-fly compliance.

columns.description: Primary key for medical certificate record
Date when medical certificate was issued

tables.description: Records individual duty periods for crew members, capturing start and end timestamps for a specific flight assignment.

columns.description: Primary key for duty log entry

tables.description: Logs regulatory or policy compliance violations attributed to crew members, including violation type and date of occurrence.

columns.description: Primary key for compliance violation record
Type or category of compliance violation

tables.description: Captures system-level audit trail of user actions across database tables for traceability and security monitoring.

columns.description: Primary key for audit log entry
Foreign key referencing user who performed action

tables.description: Records crew member enrollments in scheduled training sessions, tracking enrollment status.

columns.description: Primary key for training enrollment record
Current enrollment status for the session

tables.description: Documents simulator training sessions completed by crew for specific aircraft types, including session date and pass/fail result.

columns.description: Primary key for simulator session record
Date when simulator session took place
Outcome or result of the simulator session

tables.description: Stores expense reimbursement claims submitted by crew members, linked to expense categories with approval status.

columns.description: Primary key for expense claim record
Monetary amount of the expense claim
Current status of the expense claim

tables.description: Holds notifications dispatched to crew members with title, message body, and creation timestamp.

columns.description: Title or subject of the notification
Full text body of the notification

tables.description: Records mandatory rest periods taken by crew members between duties to ensure regulatory compliance.

columns.description: Primary key for rest period record

tables.description: Junction records assigning crew members to specific flights and rosters with a defined role and assignment status.

columns.description: Primary key for crew assignment record
Role assigned to the crew member

tables.description: Captures leave requests submitted by crew members, including leave type, date range, and approval status.

columns.description: Primary key for leave request record
Foreign key to leave type lookup
First day of the leave period
Last day of the leave period
Current approval status of leave request

tables.description: Tracks crew member availability by date for rostering and scheduling purposes.

columns.description: Current availability status of crew member

tables.description: Stores emergency contact information associated with each crew member for use in critical situations.

columns.description: Primary key for emergency contact record
Full name of the emergency contact
Phone number of the emergency contact

tables.description: Holds primary contact details for crew members including phone, email, and postal address.

columns.description: Primary key for crew contact record
Phone number of the crew member
Email address of the crew member
Physical address of the crew member

tables.description: Records aircraft type-specific ratings held by crew members, with issuance and expiry dates.

columns.description: Primary key for crew aircraft rating

tables.description: Junction table linking crew members to the professional licenses they hold, with validity dates.

columns.description: Primary key for crew license record

tables.description: Core master record for crew members containing employee number, name, rank, base assignment, hire date, and current status.

columns.description: Primary key for crew member record
First name of the crew member
Last name of the crew member
Foreign key to crew rank lookup
Date the crew member was hired

tables.description: Defines scheduled training sessions linked to a training program with date and instructor details.

columns.description: Primary key for training session record
Scheduled date of the training session

tables.description: Reference data describing training programs available to crew and their validity period in months.

columns.description: Primary key for training program record
Number of months program is valid

tables.description: Reference table listing license types that crew members may hold.
Master data for flights including flight number, origin, destination, and scheduled departure and arrival times.

columns.description: Unique identifier for a flight record
Flight number code assigned to flight

tables.description: Defines monthly roster publications by month and year, with a flag indicating whether the roster is published.

columns.description: Unique identifier for a roster period
Flag indicating if roster is published

tables.description: Reference data for aircraft types operated, with code and descriptive name.

columns.description: Full descriptive name of aircraft model

tables.description: Reference table enumerating the types of leave available to crew members.
Reference table categorizing types of expenses claimable by crew members.
Reference data for crew base locations identified by airport code and base name.

columns.description: Unique identifier for crew base location

tables.description: Reference table defining crew rank classifications such as Captain, First Officer, or Cabin Crew.

columns.description: Name of the crew rank position

views.definition:
SELECT duty_log_id,
    crew_id,
    flight_id,
    duty_start,
    duty_end
   FROM dutylog;

entity_relationships.related_entities: Flight, Roster, Training, Simulation, Compliance, Leave, Expense, Notification, Availability, MedicalCertificate, CrewLicense, CrewAircraftRating, CrewContact, CrewEmergencyContact, CrewRank, CrewBase, License, AircraftType
entity_relationships.business_meaning: Central workforce entity. A crew member belongs to one CrewRank and one CrewBase, holds many Licenses (via CrewLicense junction), qualifies for many AircraftTypes (via CrewAircraftRating junction), and has many child records across operations (1:N to all operational tables).
An operational flight is staffed by many Crew members through CrewAssignment (M:N resolved) and is tracked via DutyLog records (1:N). CrewAssignment also ties each flight to a Roster.
A roster (scheduling period) groups many CrewAssignments, each linking one Crew member to one Flight (1:N to CrewAssignment, which fans out to many Crew and many Flight).

TrainingProgram defines curricula; each TrainingSession belongs to one Program (1:N) and is attended by many Crew via TrainingEnrollment (M:N). SimulatorSession is a related but distinct pilot-proficiency activity per Crew/AircraftType.
Each simulator session is performed by one Crew member on one AircraftType (N:1 to each). Supports recurrent training and type-rating practice.
ComplianceViolation and RestPeriod are per-Crew regulatory records (N:1). AuditLog is a system-wide standalone history table capturing regulatory and operational events.
Each LeaveRequest belongs to one Crew member and references one LeaveType (N:1 each). Drives availability and roster gap analysis.
Each ExpenseClaim is filed by one Crew member and categorized by one ExpenseType (N:1 each). Subject to approval workflow.
Per-Crew alerts and messages (N:1 to Crew). One-way communication channel for rostering, compliance, and training reminders.
Per-Crew availability windows (N:1 to Crew) consumed by rostering to constrain CrewAssignment generation.

business_processes.tables: license, aircrafttype, leavetype, expensetype, crewbase, crewrank

use_cases.name: Crew Rostering and Flight Assignment
use_cases.description: Build rosters by matching crew qualifications (ratings, licenses), availability, and rest-period compliance to scheduled flights.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, crewassignment, roster, flight, crewaircraftrating, crewlicense, availability, restperiod

use_cases.name: Regulatory Compliance and Fatigue Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track duty hours, rest periods, and compliance violations per crew member to ensure adherence to aviation regulations.

use_cases.name: Training and Recertification Management
use_cases.description: Schedule training sessions, enroll crew, track completion, and manage simulator sessions for type ratings and recurrent training.
use_cases.involved_tables: trainingprogram, trainingsession, trainingenrollment, simulatorsession, crew, aircrafttype

use_cases.name: Leave and Expense Administration
use_cases.description: Process leave requests against availability and approve expense claims submitted by crew members.
use_cases.involved_tables: leaverequest, leavetype, expenseclaim, expensetype, crew, availability

use_cases.name: Crew Qualification and Document Expiry Tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor medical certificates, licenses, and aircraft ratings to flag upcoming expirations and renewal needs.
use_cases.involved_tables: medicalcertificate, crewlicense, license, crewaircraftrating, crew, notification

sample_queries.question: List all crew members qualified to operate a specific aircraft type.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name FROM crew c JOIN crewaircraftrating car ON c.crew_id = car.crew_id JOIN aircrafttype at ON car.aircraft_type_id = at.aircraft_type_id WHERE at.type_code = 'B737';

sample_queries.question: Show total duty hours and flight count per crew member for the current month.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT c.crew_id, c.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT dl.flight_id) AS flight_count, SUM(dl.duration_hours) AS total_duty_hours FROM crew c JOIN dutylog dl ON c.crew_id = dl.crew_id WHERE dl.log_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY c.crew_id, c.last_name ORDER BY total_duty_hours DESC;

sample_queries.question: Find crew members with expired or soon-to-expire medical certificates.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT c.crew_id, c.last_name, mc.expiry_date FROM crew c JOIN medicalcertificate mc ON c.crew_id = mc.crew_id WHERE mc.expiry_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '30 days' ORDER BY mc.expiry_date;

sample_queries.question: Detect rest-period violations (less than minimum required hours between duties).
sample_queries.sql: SELECT c.crew_id, c.last_name, dl1.log_date AS duty_end, dl2.log_date AS next_duty_start, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (dl2.log_date - dl1.log_date))/3600 AS hours_between FROM crew c JOIN dutylog dl1 ON c.crew_id = dl1.crew_id JOIN dutylog dl2 ON c.crew_id = dl2.crew_id WHERE dl2.log_date > dl1.log_date AND (dl2.log_date - dl1.log_date) < INTERVAL '12 hours';

sample_queries.question: Get the roster with crew assignments for a given date range showing crew rank and base.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT r.roster_id, r.start_date, r.end_date, f.flight_number, c.crew_id, c.last_name, cr.rank_name, cb.base_name FROM roster r JOIN crewassignment ca ON r.roster_id = ca.roster_id JOIN flight f ON ca.flight_id = f.flight_id JOIN crew c ON ca.crew_id = c.crew_id JOIN crewrank cr ON c.rank_id = cr.rank_id JOIN crewbase cb ON c.base_id = cb.base_id WHERE r.start_date >= '2024-01-01' AND r.end_date <= '2024-01-31' ORDER BY r.start_date, f.flight_number;

schema_patterns.description: Columns tracking creation and modification timestamps and users
